primary_subject: Anubis at the scales of Ma'at

image_requests.prompt: Anubis the jackal-headed ancient Egyptian god of death standing inside a torch-lit stone tomb, both hands gesturing toward golden scales of Ma'at, one scale holding a softly glowing human heart and the other a pristine white feather, warm amber and deep gold candlelight casting dramatic shadows, golden dust motes floating in the air, dark basalt walls with carved hieroglyphics barely visible in background, rich blacks and deep burnished gold color palette, cinematic photorealistic digital painting, 8k, subject positioned slightly right of center frame, atmospheric mist near the floor
image_requests.purpose: Hero image — Anubis and the judgment scales occupy the right 60% of the frame, serving as the unmistakable visual symbol of the Egyptian afterlife

html:
CHEAT
DEATH
why: The composition uses a deliberate split-field: the left third is swallowed in near-total darkness and owned entirely by 'CHEAT DEATH' in 158px Cinzel Black — the ancient Roman capital letterforms echo carved stone inscriptions, tying the type itself to antiquity. The right two-thirds are held by Anubis at his scales, lit from within by golden candle warmth — the exact visual the locked hook was written against, so the image and the text complete each other's meaning rather than compete. The golden text-shadow bloom on the white letterforms creates a halo that visually rhymes with the torch-gold of the scene, bridging both halves. At 168×94px, the viewer reads two things simultaneously: a bold white text mass (CHEAT DEATH) and a dark silhouetted figure with a golden glow — enough to decode 'Egyptian afterlife secret' in under a second.
title: (NO ADS) Ancient Egypt's Book of the Dead | A Calm Journey Through the Afterlife | 1 Hour Sleep Story
